title: DNS options taken from host may break host resolution in containers
body:
### Issue Description

I've deployed a set of multiple pods with `podman kube play ...`, which use some inter-pod communications. As an example, consider the classic wordpress-database-setup, **but with both containers in a distinct pod**.
To be able to connect from pod A to pod B, the provided DNS service is used to resolve the particular host.
That's basically running fine, so I'm not going in more detail here.

However, I discovered that this resolution was highly unreliable, only return the host's IP with every other request. Meanwhile it has become clear why that is.

When creating the containers, podman obviously takes the `/etc/resolv.conf` from the local host, which is this
```
nameserver 10.10.0.2
nameserver 10.10.0.11
options rotate timeout:1
```
and just inserts it's own nameserver on top:
```
nameserver 10.89.0.1
nameserver 10.10.0.2
nameserver 10.10.0.11
options rotate timeout:1
```

Due to the option "rotate", the container is unable to properly resolve the other's pod name reliably, because it also uses external DNS hosts to resolve internal pod names.
Unfortunately, I can neither remove that option from the local host, nor did I find a way to tell podman (via `/etc/containers/containers.conf`) to drop the options altogether, when creating the container.

So, unless I've overseen the possibility to do so, I would consider this a bug ;)

### Steps to reproduce the issue

Steps to reproduce the issue
1. Define "options rotate" in your local `/etc/resolv.conf`
2. Create a new pod/container with `podman kube play ...` 
3. Enter the container and validate the content of `/etc/resolv.conf`


### Describe the results you received

podman blindly copies the problematic "rotate" DNS option, and no workaround seems possible to explicitly prevent the copy of the options


### Describe the results you expected

podman should either filter any "harmful" DNS options, or at least provide a way to override the options within `/etc/containers/containers.conf`.
As far as I understood, it currently only allows to **add** options.

comments.body:
Please retry with the latest version, I think this is the bug were we added the host resolvers by accident.
If dns is used it should only include the aardvark-dns ip and aardvark-dns will forward external requests.
